mcqs.question: What is the average time complexity of search, insert, and delete operations in a Binary Search Tree?
mcqs.explanation: The average time complexity of search, insert, and delete operations in a Binary Search Tree is O(log n) because the tree is roughly balanced, allowing for efficient operations.

mcqs.question: What is the property of a Binary Search Tree that ensures efficient search, insert, and delete operations?

options.text: All values in the left subtree are greater than the node's value
All values in the right subtree are less than the node's value
All values in the left subtree are less than the node's value, and all values in the right subtree are greater than the node's value
All values in the left subtree are equal to the node's value

mcqs.explanation: The property of a Binary Search Tree that ensures efficient search, insert, and delete operations is that all values in the left subtree are less than the node's value, and all values in the right subtree are greater than the node's value.

mcqs.question: What is the purpose of the 'deleteNode' function in a Binary Search Tree?

options.text: To insert a new value into the tree
To search for a value in the tree
To delete a value from the tree

mcqs.explanation: The 'deleteNode' function is used to delete a value from the Binary Search Tree.

mcqs.question: What is the time complexity of the 'searchNode' function in a Binary Search Tree?
mcqs.explanation: The time complexity of the 'searchNode' function in a Binary Search Tree is O(log n) because the tree is roughly balanced, allowing for efficient search operations.

mcqs.question: What is the space complexity of a Binary Search Tree?
mcqs.explanation: The space complexity of a Binary Search Tree is O(n) because each node in the tree requires additional memory to store its value and child pointers.

mcqs.question: What is the purpose of the 'createNode' function in a Binary Search Tree?

options.text: To insert a new value into the tree
To search for a value in the tree
To delete a value from the tree
To create a new node with a given value

mcqs.explanation: The 'createNode' function is used to create a new node with a given value in the Binary Search Tree.

mcqs.question: What is the difference between a full Binary Search Tree and an empty Binary Search Tree?

options.text: A full Binary Search Tree has no nodes, while an empty Binary Search Tree has at least one node
A full Binary Search Tree has at least one node, while an empty Binary Search Tree has no nodes
A full Binary Search Tree has all nodes with two children, while an empty Binary Search Tree has no nodes
A full Binary Search Tree has all nodes with one child, while an empty Binary Search Tree has no nodes

mcqs.explanation: A full Binary Search Tree has all nodes with two children, while an empty Binary Search Tree has no nodes.

mcqs.question: What is the time complexity of the 'insertNode' function in a Binary Search Tree?
mcqs.explanation: The time complexity of the 'insertNode' function in a Binary Search Tree is O(log n) because the tree is roughly balanced, allowing for efficient insert operations.
